opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My right calf is throbbing with a deep ache, and it has swollen up twice the size of my left leg since yesterday.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden painful swelling and redness of one calf that came on over the last day, now making it hard to walk.

symptom_narrative.duration: about a day of deep aching calf pain followed rapidly by swelling, warmth and tightness
symptom_narrative.character: a deep, dull, throbbing ache in the calf muscles, with the skin feeling warm and tight
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: painful enough that he is limping heavily and shifting his weight off the leg
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the right calf/lower leg, swollen up to the knee
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on over the last 24 hours and has been steadily worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: resting and keeping the leg still
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: warmth and redness over the calf and a feeling of tightness; denies chest pain, breathlessness, or coughing up blood
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: independent but now limping heavily and struggling to weight-bear on the leg

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; recently returned from a 14-hour long-haul flight three days ago

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: worried about the pain and swelling and whether it's serious; lives alone so concerned about coping if he can't walk

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes symptoms clearly and in order

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported low-grade temperature, not a clinician measurement
